_comment: EXAMPLE managed policy — the version IT/DevOps would push to every machine (via MDM / a managed settings path), not a file engineers edit. Managed settings sit ABOVE user, project, and local scopes and CANNOT be overridden by them. VERIFY every key below against your installed Claude Code version before shipping: (1) the managed settings file location; (2) exact key names for locking modes and marketplaces; (3) permission-rule string syntax. Deny rules from all scopes merge, so the org deny list here always applies on top of each repo's own.

permissions._comment_rules: Org-wide floor. These deny rules are added to whatever each project already denies — they cannot be removed by a project or a local file.
permissions._comment_modes: Take the dangerous escape hatches off the table for the whole fleet. bypassPermissions skips every prompt; auto mode hands the safety decision to a background classifier. The value is the string "disable", and these keys live under permissions.

_comment_rules_ownership: Left OFF on purpose: allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly would forbid teams from defining ANY allow/ask/deny of their own. We want teams to still own their project settings.json — the managed deny list above merges on top regardless.
_comment_supply_chain: Contain the skills/MCP attack surface (the AST10 lesson). Only hooks shipped in managed settings run; plugin marketplaces are blocked; sideload flags are rejected so nobody can point Claude Code at an unvetted source from the CLI.
